sglang.title: fix: mistral embedding regression fix
sglang.author: dougyster
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Embedding models using LlamaTokenizerFast (e.g. intfloat/e5-mistral-7b-instruct) regressed in cosine similarity from ~1.0 to ~0.33 after the transformers v5.3.0 upgrade (#17784). The regression was identified by bisecting between v0.5.9 (good) and v0.5.10rc0 (bad), pinning the first bad c

sglang.title: [5/n] Lora support cuda graph
sglang.author: yushengsu-thu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
MoE LoRA inference does not support CUDA graph because the forward path dynamically allocates intermediate tensors (torch.empty()) on every call. CUDA graph requires fixed tensor addresses between capture and replay, so these dynamic allocations break graph replay.
Modifications

Pre-allo

sglang.title: [diffusion] Default NVFP4 to CUTLASS and add all-model shape benchmarks
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

switch the default diffusion NVFP4 backend from comfy-kitchen to the generic JIT CUTLASS path
keep comfy-kitchen available behind SGLANG_DIFFUSION_NVFP4_LINEAR_BACKEND=comfy
wire SGLANG_DIFFUSION_FLASHINFER_FP4_GEMM_BACKEND into the diffusion NVFP4 fallback path
add a standalone NVFP4 scale

sglang.title: Add dsv3 router gemm benchmark on blackwell
sglang.author: harrisonlimh
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Comparing dsv3_router_gemm performance between flashinfer and the current sglang kernel to optimize performance (#14453)

Modifications

Add accuracy and performance benchmark script to compare dsv3_router_gemm from sglang kernel and flashinfer

Accuracy Tests
Confirmed accuracy tests pa

sglang.title: [diffusion] Validate attention backend for Ring Attention in USPAttention
sglang.author: yeahdongcn
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Found this issue in a MUSA container where MATE is not installed. Although the validation in ServerArgs._adjust_attention_backend() checks at the string level and will pick fa for Ring Attention (if attention backend is not set), when actually resolving the attention backend via get_attn

sglang.title: Add LFM2-VL (Liquid Foundation Model 2 Vision-Language) support
sglang.author: tugot17
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for the LFM2-VL vision-language architecture, combining a SigLip2 vision encoder (NaFlex variable-resolution) with the LFM2 hybrid language model.
Example model: LFM2.5-VL-1.6B
How to run
sglang serve --model-path LiquidAI/LFM2.5-VL-1.6B

# Also tested on TP=2
sglang serve --mod

sglang.title: [VLM] Chunk-aware ViT encoding with per-image cache and lazy device transfer
sglang.author: yhyang201
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Per-image embedding cache: Switch multimodal embedding cache granularity from per-request (combine_hashes(all_items)) to per-image (item.hash), improving cache reuse under LRU eviction and avoiding full recomputation when any single image is evicted.
Chunk-aware ViT encoding: Only encode

sglang.title: [Speculative Decoding] Add FA4-based Spec Support
sglang.author: narutolhy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
FA4 (FP4 Attention) significantly reduces memory footprint and improves throughput, especially for large-scale and multimodal workloads.
However, FA4 is currently not compatible with the speculative decoding pipeline, which limits its adoption in latency-sensitive scenarios where speculat

sglang.title: [Feature] Add Reasoning Tokens Usage
sglang.author: Muqi1029
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
SGLang currently returns token usage information, but the reasoning_tokens field is always 0, which makes it unusable as a statistical metric. This is problematic since reasoning_tokens is an important signal for analysis and monitoring.
You can see the following result using latest(main

sglang.title: Relax spec decoding accuracy threshold to fix flaky test
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

test_gsm8k in test_standalone_speculative_decoding.py uses assertGreater(score, 0.7) (strict >), causing flaky failures when score lands exactly on the boundary
Observed in CI:

Run 23974687870: AssertionError: 0.7 not greater than 0.7
Run 23973150935: AssertionError: 0.69 not greater than

sglang.title: Fix killall_sglang missing the main sglang serve process
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
killall_sglang (CI cleanup script) uses regex pattern matching on /proc/PID/cmdline to find and kill SGLang processes. However, the main server process launched via sglang serve is not matched by the current patterns, because its cmdline looks like:
/usr/local/bin/python3 /usr/local/bin/s

sglang.title: Fix Python 3.11 f-string lint error in deepgemm Blackwell benchmark
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fix two Python 3.11-incompatible f-strings in benchmark/kernels/deepseek/benchmark_deepgemm_dsv3_router_gemm_blackwell.py.
This is an isolated hotfix branch cut from main, independent of the current working branch.
The syntax issue was introduced by #17707 and caused pre-commit hooks like ch

sglang.title: Align diffusion nightly presets and broaden skill discovery
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Made with codex
Summary

align diffusion nightly comparison config, benchmark presets, and docs around the LTX-2 nightly case
add missing diffusion opportunity-discovery guidance for fused QK norm + RoPE, Nunchaku fused GELU MLP, and existing overlap families
move the Z-Image fused modulation regres

sglang.title: [Benchmark] Add auto benchmark tool with YAML-driven server flag search and canonical dataset format
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Made with Codex
This work replaces the SGLang performance tuning cookbook with a single natural-language prompt. You just tell the AI agent your GPU count, model name (or path), target SLA (max TTFT / TPOT), and your real-world data path — everything else (dataset conversion, search space

sglang.title: [diffusion] CI: improve diffusion comparison benchmark setting for realistic perf and auto-discover ut
sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Increase num_inference_steps from 2 → 20 for Wan2.2-T2V-A14B and Wan2.2-I2V-A14B benchmark cases
With only 2 steps, denoising appeared faster than encoding (distorted), and the dual-DiT second model (boundary_ratio=0.875) barely got exercised
20 steps gives ~17 high-noise + 3 low-noise step

sglang.title: fix: add missing f-string prefixes in warning and assert messages
sglang.author: Ricardo-M-L
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Add missing f prefix in phi4mm.py where {name} is printed literally in warning message
Add missing f prefix in memory_pool_host.py where {layout} is printed literally in assert message

Motivation
Without the f prefix, these messages show literal {name} and {layout} text instead of actual v

sglang.title: feat: CI auto-bisect workflow for automated regression analysis
sglang.author: Kangyan-Zhou
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Adds a new GitHub Actions workflow that triggers after every scheduled PR Test (Nvidia) run on main
Automatically analyzes failing tests using a lightweight GitHub API analyzer (~20 API calls) and Claude Sonnet 4.5 (with adaptive thinking) to classify each failure as: code regression, flaky

sglang.title: [AMD]: Support MLA with nhead<16 and FP8 KV cache for TP=8 (Kimi K2.5…
sglang.author: ZiguanWang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Modifications

Support AITER MLA for num_heads < 16 (e.g., TP=8 with Kimi K2.5 giving 8 heads/rank). Uses head-repeat to expand to 16 heads before calling the AITER MLA decode kernel, then contracts the output back. This reuses the existing optimized gqa_ratio=16 ASM kernel without requi

sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Remove TestToolChoiceLfm2Moe from test_tool_choice.py.
LiquidAI/LFM2-8B-A1B is unreliable for tool calling — test_tool_choice_specific_function_non_streaming flakes on it regularly (e.g. https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/actions/runs/23984641093/job/69954483711).
This class uses the same

vllm.title: [Bugfix][CPU] Fix macOS compatibility broken by #36487
vllm.author: 2imi9
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
PR #36487 ("Replace OMP initialization") introduced unconditional calls to Linux-only APIs that break vLLM's CPU support on macOS:

os.sched_getaffinity — does not exist on macOS
lscpu — Linux-only command

This was flagged by @hmellor in #36487 (review comment).
Changes
vllm/utils/ompmultip

vllm.title: [Perf][GDN] Align TMA usage with upstream FLA
vllm.author: arpera
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Align is_tma_supported in vLLM's GDN kernels with upstream FLA behavior: TMA is now disabled by default and requires FLA_USE_TMA=1 to enable.
vLLM's copy of FLA GDN kernels was forked before fla-org/flash-linear-attention#607 / 2eade97, so is_tma_supported is unconditionally True on SM90+ (H
